programmes.show_description: Mike's come off his bike and is experiencing altered sensations in his legs. Luke's also had a serious bike crash which has left a piece of his bone lying out on the road.
Dad-of-two Tony is rushed to King's after being badly hurt in a road traffic collision. Eric, who's 85, has ongoing stomach problems. Jim, who's 79, has difficulty breathing.
A young man is treated for a severe reaction to a recreational drug, while 65-year-old Christine from Camberwell is brought in to King's by her friends after banging her head.
The tour of North Yorkshire continues. The campers visit the tranquil, eco-friendly campsite, The Hideaway at Baxby Manor, where they forage for their food and take to the skies.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Tina and her daughter Kay welcome their rivals to the Foxhall Village Guest House in Blackpool. But the shared facilities don't wash with their guests.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Dumfries and Galloway, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find an electric organ and a vintage oil dispenser in Maggie's barns, which are brimming with junk.
Maureen wants to retire to the charming, whitewashed town of Mojácar on Spain's Costa de Almería. Can Scarlette Douglas find Maureen's perfect place, with a terrace and sea views, for £90k?
Swindon and Marlborough. The first host, super-confident software sales rep Simon, hopes to impress with a night full of charm and hearty, home-cooked food. (Ep1/5)
Swindon and Marlborough. Business consultant Linda serves a game-based menu including pheasant and venison, and pastry swans. But Simon puts the cat among the pigeons... (Ep2/5)
Swindon and Marlborough. Parisienne Saleha serves up a vegetarian meal... with a side of steak tartare. (Ep3/5)
Swindon and Marlborough. Expectations are high as drama teacher Ben serves up a Middle Eastern and Asian menu. Simon raises eyebrows when he calls the group 'vanilla'. (Ep4/5)
Swindon and Marlborough. Ex-military police officer Debs treats her guests to a medieval feast of local legends, including 'flying monk' and 'lamb leg of barmaid-gobbled tiger'. (Ep5/5)
Jenny and Johnny welcome their rivals to BnB at 43 in Richmond, North Yorkshire. But there's a mark on Kelly and Serena's toilet. At dinner, Debra and Jamie reveal their regal connections.
The second visit of the week is to Sutton Park in Blackpool, run by Stewart and Gill. Kelly and Serena are blown away by the bling, but Debra and Jamie have cleanliness concerns.
Debra and Jamie host at Tottington Manor in Edburton, West Sussex. But Gill doesn't like the countryside setting or her room, and low scores at feedback lead to cries of foul play.
The final visit is to Flynn's Pub and Guesthouse in Hackney, east London, where friends Serena and Kelly claim to have the cleanest and most stylish rooms at a price unmatched in the area.
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for the last time. After dramatic discoveries and cutting comments, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Citroen HY Van: Former England footballers Bryan Robson and Wes Brown join Tim and Fuzz in Manchester as they restore a Citroen HY van belonging to a family torn apart by tragedy.
Vauxhall Cavalier Turbo: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Stockport to rescue a 1994 Cavalier Turbo for Darren, who was left tetraplegic after a horrific accident.
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are in Bath with first-time buyers Laura and Phil, and downsizing duo Kerrie and Dave, who hope to move closer to their children.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer revisit Dawn and Paul, who were torn between renovating their Huddersfield home or relocating. Since then, there have been big changes...
State-of-the-art drones explore Britain's great country houses. At Castle Howard, the estate launches a re-wilding project as it renovates for the spring season. (Ep2/4)
This moving episode looks at the start and end of life, as well as the joys and fears of living alone. Graham, who's 75, has been attacked. Mum Cheryl has two poorly boys.
On a busy Saturday night in A&E at King's College Hospital, staff must juggle the needs of elderly patients and sick children with patients who are drunk or violent.
The heli-medics help a teen motorcyclist who's been hit by a car and catapulted into the air. A holiday fitness workout ends in CPR. And a lorry driver is trapped in his cabin.
This moving episode looks at the start and end of life, as well as the joys and fears of living alone. Graham, who's 75, has been attacked. Mum Cheryl has two poorly boys.
On a busy Saturday night in A&E at King's College Hospital, staff must juggle the needs of elderly patients and sick children with patients who are drunk or violent.
The camping connoisseurs visit the stunning setting of Masons Campsite, Skipton, where they have an exhilarating stay involving river swimming, fishing, cricket and croissants.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Raqeeb and Raj hope to pip their rivals to the post with their slick operation and competitive pricing at the Douglas Hotel in Hartlepool. But will a dinner ding-dong scupper their chances?

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In the Lake District, Simon O'Brien has ambitious plans to turn a pile of old slate into an unusual lamp, while Henry Cole upcycles the seat from an old pony and trap.
Golf fan Peggy can't think of anywhere else she'd rather buy a holiday home than on Spain's Costa del Sol. Danni Menzies tees up five properties for £115k, but will any score a hole in one?
Cardiff area. On the first night, 26-year-old IT architect Anna serves up classic British cooking with a twist. But her jus proves elusive and her posset provokes confusion. (Ep1/5)
Cardiff area. Charlotte splashes the cash on luxury ingredients. But her floodlit pink house and fancy champagne raise eyebrows, and Anna compares her soufflé to scrambled egg. (Ep2/5)
Cardiff area. On the third night of the competition, beautician Claire serves up her favourite dishes - on planks. Because, for some reason, she really, really loves planks... (Ep3/5)
Cardiff area. Part-time football referee Scott plans a menu of family favourites, with cheese in every course. But tension is running high after last night's drama. (Ep4/5)
Cardiff area. Restaurateur Giovanni plans a menu from his native Italy. But Charlotte wants revenge after he criticised her night, and the other guests think he's trying too hard. (Ep5/5)
The competition kicks off at The Unicorn Inn in Ludlow, where Zoe and Lee hope that their glamorous, fairy-tale rooms will become the stuff of legend. And Zoe wows the guests at breakfast.
The second visit is to the East Walls Hotel in Chichester, which owners Celia and Damon describe as 'bespoke'. Their guests go crazy for the continental, but there's loo brush controversy.
There's toilet brush controversy for the second time in the week. And the battle of the bangers continues, as ambitious Jake and Chris welcome their rivals to The Wheatsheaf in Cheshire.
The final visit is to Shawries Hotel in Blackpool, run by Allison and Dave. The décor takes Jake and Chris back to their childhood, but Celia and Damon aren't fans of the 1980s look.
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for the last time. After strong opinions are aired and differences debated, the winner is announced.
MGB GT: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend take on their 100th motor and their first electric conversion: a 1978 MGB GT, which Caroline promised her late dad she'd get completed.
Renault Alpine GTA: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Dorset to fix up a 1987 Renault Alpine GTA, which desperately needs some TLC.
In Greater Manchester, Kirstie Allsopp helps Jacinta and Marco, who've outgrown their home. And Phil Spencer's with Olivia and Amy, who want to put down roots in the north.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Keeley and Terence's family of five are bursting out of their three-bed, one-bath house in Woodford in London. Can Phil trump Kirstie's layout master plan?
In Lincolnshire, high-flyers Zara and Giuliano set out to build a 400-square-metre malthouse-inspired home, but run into problems from the get-go. Can they get the project off the ground?
A young girl arrives at King's College Hospital seriously injured after being hit by a car and a bus. Plus, a look at other pedestrians who've ended up as patients in A&E.
This episode focuses on men of all ages facing illness and their fears. Arthur, who's 79, has stomach pains but isn't one to complain. Tom, 20, is having trouble breathing.
